name: Delice Aux Noisettes
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Whip half a cup of heavy cream in a chilled bowl or over ice until soft peaks form. Add confectioners' sugar and vanilla, then beat until stiff peaks form.
3. Line a strainer with cheesecloth and set over a bowl.
4. Place hazelnuts on a tray in the oven for about 10 minutes. Do not let them burn. Remove and skin the nuts. Keep half a cup of the warm nuts aside.
5. In a stainless steel or aluminum saucepan, heat half a cup of sugar with three tablespoons water to boiling.
6. Add a pinch of cream of tartar and continue cooking until a light brown color is achieved.
7. Lightly oil a baking sheet with one to two tablespoons of oil.
8. When the syrup is a light brown, pour in the warm nuts and stir with a wooden spoon to coat evenly. Pour onto the oiled baking sheet.
9. Let cool for 10 minutes until hardened.
10. Grind remaining nuts and half a cup of sugar in a food processor into nut butter. This should take from six to ten minutes. The oils must emerge, resembling peanut butter.
11. Chop the cooled nougatine coarsely with a knife into pieces small enough to eat.
12. Mix cream and eggs together, add to nut butter, and pour into the quiche shell.
13. Sprinkle nougatine over the top.
14. Bake for 25-35 minutes until browned and set. Serve at room temperature with dollops of whipped cream on top using a tablespoon. Decorate with whipped cream piped through a pastry bag.
